problem_statement: The order-management service is experiencing response times above 30 seconds. Multiple customers report timeouts when viewing order history.

app-logs.text: WARN connection pool exhausted — waited 28s for available connection
WARN HikariPool-1 — pool stats: active=100, idle=0, waiting=37, total=100
ERROR SqlTransientConnectionException: could not acquire connection from pool within 30000ms

db-metrics.text: Active connections: 100/100 max_connections. 40 connections held by user 'analytics_svc'

Long-running query (pid 8842): SELECT * FROM orders JOIN line_items ON orders.id = line_items.order_id JOIN products ON line_items.product_id = products.id WHERE orders.created_at > '2025-01-01' — running for 47 minutes, state: active
analytics_svc connections breakdown: 40 active, 0 idle. No statement_timeout configured for role.
Top query by total_time: SELECT * FROM orders JOIN line_items ... — 0 index scans, sequential scan on orders (12M rows)

infra-metrics.text: CPU usage: 78%, Memory: 62%, Disk I/O wait: 45%
Disk read throughput: 450 MB/s (baseline: 50 MB/s) — sequential scan detected

network-logs.text: Traffic between order-service and postgres-primary: normal volume, no drops
postgres-primary.internal resolved to 10.0.2.15 in 1ms — no errors

expected_root_cause: An unoptimized analytics query (full sequential scan on 12M rows, no index usage) running without a statement_timeout consumed 40 of 100 database connections for 47+ minutes. This exhausted the connection pool, causing the order-management service to queue requests until they timed out at 30 seconds.
